It’s reported that Hamilton had explored ‘every way’ to try to attend the ceremony, but the timeline proved to be too difficult. Shanghai is a 12-and-a-half-hour flight from Los Angeles, making it impossible for him to get there in time.

Speaking earlier in the week, Hamilton said: "I'll FaceTime with Joe [Kosinski, director] and Jerry [Bruckheimer, lead producer] when they're there, which will be cool.

‌



View 2 Images The team behind F1: The Movie accept the Best Sound Oscar. (Image: Kevin Winter/Getty Images)

"I'm incredibly proud, and I never ever thought in a million years that that would be the outcome of the work that we're doing over the past years.

"It's amazing to see. I don't know if it's the sport, but to see how much promotion there is around the world, to see the buzz, to see new people getting excited for the sport, in the way that so many of us were growing up.

Article continues below

"It's really great to see that that is expanding. And on top of that, I'm still here, still to be a part of it and witness it."

Hamilton's third-place finish moves him up to fourth overall in the rankings. He will next be on the track in the Japanese Grand Prix on Sunday, 29 March.

Sports.title: Tyson Fury vs Anthony Joshua 'agreed' as stunning details emerge
Sports.content:
Tyson Fury vs Anthony Joshua 'agreed' as stunning details emerge

A showdown between the pair appeared to be off the cards following Joshua's near-death experience in December, but it has now been revealed that the fight could be back on

View Image Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua could fight this year

Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua have reportedly agreed to meet in a mouthwatering heavyweight showdown - as major details have been revealed.

‌



Back in December, reports emerged that the pair were due to finally settle their long-time feud in the ring in 2026. However, just days after his knockout win over Jake Paul this past Christmas, Joshua was involved in a tragic car crash in Nigeria, which resulted in the deaths of his two close friends Sina Ghami and Lateef 'Latz' Ayodele.

‌



There were fears that Joshua might not ever fight again after he cheated death, but the former two-time heavyweight world champion is now back doing some light training amid talks of a potential return to the ring this summer. 'AJ' has been mooted to face ex-rival Dillian Whyte before chasing a clash with Fury.

‌



According to boxing journalist Gareth A Davies, both Fury and Joshua have agreed to meet in the ring. Speaking on talkSPORT, Davies revealed that the fight is 'signed' before claiming it's been 'agreed'. "The Fury-Joshua fight is signed," he said. "Okay, it is signed in the background. I've got that on good authority."

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Jake Paul brutally mocked over unfortunate situation on stage with Donald Trump READ MORE :

Mike Tyson vs Floyd Mayweather boxing fight to have unusual rule READ MORE :

Davies added that the showcase will be live on Netflix and will likely take place either 'late in the year' or 'next year' - with Joshua likely to return to action in either 'June or July.' "It's with the big money people. It'll be on Netflix." Davies added: "It's on the cards and it's going to happen."

Netflix are due to air Fury's comeback on April 11. Last year, the 'Gypsy King' stunned fans after announcing his retirement from the sport of boxing, only to reverse his decision just months later. The 37-year-old is scheduled to take on dangerous Russian knockout artist Arslanbek Makhmudov at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London. Netflix have emerged as a real powerhouse in the world of boxing in recent times, having aired showdowns such as Paul vs Joshua, Canelo Alvarez vs Terence Crawford, Katie Taylor vs Amanda Serrano and Paul vs Mike Tyson.

Donald Trump endorses Jake Paul for political career amid injury update READ MORE :

Joshua is is closing in on a return to full training ahead of a potential return to the squared-circle later this year. Last month, the Brit's promoter, Eddie Hearn had revealed that an all-British heavyweight showdown between Fury and Joshua could still happen despite his star client's brush with death.

Article continues below

"Originally the plan with AJ was for him to fight in March and then fight Tyson Fury in August," Hearn told Boxing Scene. "He's not going to fight Tyson Fury next; he's going to come back, I believe, in late summer. Physically he's not yet in a position to return so I'm planning and he's just resting and preparing.

"I'm looking at options to get him back in the ring in July but we will only know if that's a real possibility when he returns to camp which will hopefully be in the next couple of weeks Every fight is dangerous coming off what he's been through. We are open to the Tyson Fury fight but more likely at the end of the year or early in 2027."

Sports.title: Jannik Sinner's F1 Chinese Grand Prix decision as Indian Wells winner reveals sacrifice made
Sports.content:
Jannik Sinner's F1 Chinese Grand Prix decision as Indian Wells winner reveals sacrifice made

Jannik Sinner missed watching Kimi Antonelli win the Chinese Grand Prix as he beat Daniil Medvedev to win his first Indian Wells title

View 2 Images Jannik Sinner won at Indian Wells on Sunday. (Image: Getty Images)

Jannik Sinner admitted that he had to snub watching the Chinese Grand Prix to secure Indian Wells success. The world number two defeated Daniil Medvedev in straight sets to emulate Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic by completing the collection of hard court tournaments.

‌



However, the 24-year-old made the claim that he had to resist his urges and desires, like staying up late to watch the F1 in Shanghai as it would have affected his tennis. Sinner joined the exclusive group after previously winning the Australian Open and US Open, as well as all six Masters 1,000 titles before adding Indian Wells silverware to his, already, remarkable collection.

‌



The Italian required tiebreaks to overcome Medvedev and in the match-winning set, he disclosed that he had to shake off fatigue to power through.

‌



Carlos Alcaraz hates playing 'nightmare' Brit whose Wimbledon antics attracted fury READ MORE :

Tennis trio were banned for 37 years in total as new match-fixing scandal grips the sport READ MORE :

He said: "I tried to shake it off and after I was loose again. I held back a little bit so that's a point I would like to improve.

"I felt like I could have played slightly more aggressive. I wanted to watch the Formula One but it was midnight.

"I'm not capable of going to sleep very late and playing tennis the next day so very good discipline. Congrats Kimi (Antonelli)."

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Sinner was forced to miss Kimi Antonelli claim maximum points in the second race of the season, beating Mercedes teammate George Russell - winner of the opener in Melbourne.

The Italian was forced to learn the result after the event in Shanghai as Los Angeles is 15 hours behind China.

‌



It was British-heavy podium with Lewis Hamilton continuing his return to form to take third place. Sinner, meanwhile, has recovered from Australian Open disappointment where he was knocked out in a semi-final thriller with Novak Djokovic.

He defeated Learner Tien then Alexander Zverev in the semi-final en route to his Indian Wells success, not dropping a set in his path to glory.

View 2 Images Jannik Sinner bounced back from his Australian Open disappointment, romping to glory at Indian Wells. (Image: Getty Images)

Article continues below

Sinner added: "It was a tough tournament. In my mind I knew this was the only hardcourt tournament of the big ones that I haven't won so I'm very happy how I handled it.

"Seeing Daniil playing at this level is important for tennis. I feel like when he plays at his best he's very tough to beat as we saw today, but I'm very happy.

“Tennis needs him. He has a very unique style of playing, and seeing him back at this level is great. He’s improving a lot and is very aggressive. He has two different game styles when he serves and when he returns, so you have to face that.”

Sports.title: Matt Fitzpatrick left heartbroken as "good friend" costs him £3.4million payday
Sports.content:
Matt Fitzpatrick left heartbroken as "good friend" costs him £3.4million payday

Matt Fitzpatrick had a classy response to his devastating loss at the Players Championship

View 2 Images Matt Fitzpatrick was dramatically denied victory at the Players Championship (Image: James Gilbert, Getty Images)

Matt Fitzpatrick narrowly missed out on The Players Championship trophy to his "good friend" and TGL teammate Cameron Young. It was a bitter pill to swallow but the British star responded to his disappointment with typical grace.

‌



Young birdied the 17th hole and then two-putted from 9 1⁄2 feet on the 18th green to secure the first place prize of $4.5 million (£3.4m) at the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass. He was playing alongside TGL teammate Fitzpatrick, who had been leading Young until the 17th hole.

‌



Young clinched victory in a thrilling final hole, with his understated gesture to Fitzpatrick noticed just before the end. Despite the crushing defeat and having to contend with jeering from spectators outside the ropes, Fitzpatrick was full of admiration for the experience of playing alongside his friend, who offered a different perspective on the contentious behaviour of the fans.

‌



"I love Cam," Fitzpatrick said after his narrow defeat. "He's such a nice guy. He's so laid back; it's so nice. Always knew we were going to have a good day today, just obviously both comfortable around each other, and it was."

Rory McIlroy showed vulnerable side with 'heartbroken' admission after Players Championship READ MORE :

Speaking to NBC on the 18th green, Fitzpatrick added that the 28 year old American is also a great teammate. He still claimed a solo second place finish behind Young, good for a little over £2m.

"I've got a ton of respect for Cam," he continued. "He's a good friend and teammate in TGL as well," Fitzpatrick added.

‌



"We've gone really well. I knew it was going to be a nice pairing today. When you're coming down the stretch you want to be with someone that you're comfortable with and I felt we're gonna have a good day. It was exciting."

Young, who shattered Fitzpatrick's dreams with a birdie on the iconic island 17th hole and then triumphed with a par on the 18th, gave a shout out to his playing partner "He's a great guy," Young remarked after his friend missed an eight-foot par putt which would have led to a three-hole playoff.

View 2 Images Fitzpatrick congratulated Young after the win (Image: Eston Parker/ISI Photos, ISI Photos via Getty Images)

‌



"We've gotten to be around each other a decent bit through TGL, through our team, New York, and he is an excellent playing partner," the American added. "He plays quick. He's a very polite, very kind guy and enjoyable to talk to when you get the odd moment to share a few words walking around out there.

"So a very, very comfortable pairing for me, and I think you probably saw it with both of our golf. We had every right to both be nervous and found ourselves in some tricky spots, but I feel like for the both of us I think it was a comfortable one."

Level on the leaderboard heading into the 18th hole, the two golfers chatted to help calm their nerves. "We were just saying stuff, I think, to just kind of quiet our own minds," Young explained.

Article continues below

"In those moments there's a lot running through your head, and for me to have [caddie] Kyle, one of my best friends, on the bag sitting there, it's a really nice time to have something a little bit lighter in terms of conversation.

"I could have stared at my feet walking all the way down the fairway or we could have looked around and had a little bit of a laugh. I think at the moment option two was a good call."

Sports.title: Adam Peaty given reality check months after Olympic champion married Holly Ramsay
Sports.content:
Adam Peaty given reality check months after Olympic champion married Holly Ramsay

Triple Olympic champion Adam Peaty took part in his first long-course competition since the Paris 2024 Olympics

View 3 Images Adam Peaty and Holly Ramsay married in 2025 (Image: Kym Illman, Getty Images)

Adam Peaty experienced a reality check as he returned to competitive swimming for the first time since tying the knot with Holly Ramsay. The triple Olympic champion came fourth at the Edinburgh International Swim Meet in the men's 100m breaststroke, his speciality event.

‌



The 31 year old was fastest at the 50m turn. However, he lost pace on the final stretch, recording a time of 1:00.74.

‌



Filip Nowacki clinched the race in 1:00.26, with Gregory Butler (1:00.37) and Max Morgan (1:00.54) securing second and third respectively. Peaty was some way off his world record of 56.88, which he clocked at the 2019 World Championships in South Korea.

‌



Some rustiness was expected from Peaty, considering the length of his absence from competitive action. The race was his first long-course event since the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris, where he won silver.

Adam Peaty reignites sad family feud on Mother's Day as wife Holly gushes over her mum READ MORE :

Holly Ramsay and Adam Peaty give glimpse at marital home after aunt brands him a 'tool' READ MORE :

Peaty married Holly, the daughter of famed chef Gordon Ramsay, in a lavish ceremony at Bath Abbey in December. The nuptials were surrounded by rumours of a family dispute, which allegedly intensified after Peaty's mother, Caroline, wasn't invited to the bride's hen party.

Adam and Holly appear in the new Netflix documentary series 'Being Gordon Ramsay', which tracks the Michelin-starred chef over nine months as he prepares to open three restaurants, a bar and a cooking academy in London.

‌



View 3 Images Peaty returned to swimming action in Edinburgh (Image: Ian MacNicol, Getty Images)

The series followed the couple as they revealed their engagement to the Ramsay family and featured footage from their engagement party. In the second episode, Gordon praised his son-in-law, saying: "Holly's partner is an amazing young man called Adam Peaty. He's a gent, an absolute thoroughbred. To see her walk down the aisle is going to be pretty incredible."

Adam and Holly shared their engagement news on Instagram in September 2024. The episode showed the Ramsay family's reaction to the announcement.

‌



Gordon and his wife, Tana, discussed how they "couldn't be happier" to welcome Peaty and his son into the family. The Staffordshire swimmer was previously in a relationship with Eirianedd Munro and the pair share a five year old son called George-Anderson.

View 3 Images Adam and Holly appear in a new Gordon Ramsay documentary (Image: Mark Sutton - Formula 1, Formula 1 via Getty Images)

During the episode, Peaty recalled the "daunting" moment he sought Holly's parents' blessing to marry her, and said: "It turned into a very wonderful moment that I'll remember for the rest of my life."

Article continues below

Episode four covers the couple's engagement party, with Peaty disclosing that he knew he wanted to marry Holly as she "brings me a peace that I've never had in all aspects in my life".

Peaty, a two-time Olympic breaststroke champion, is scheduled to compete at two major events this summer, the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow and the European Championships in Paris. He recently visited Australia's Gold Coast for a training camp.

Sports.title: England vs Uruguay and Japan tickets from £35 as VIP packages still available for Wembley
Sports.content:
England vs Uruguay and Japan tickets from £35 as VIP packages still available for Wembley

England will play Uruguay on March 27 and Japan on March 31 at Wembley Stadium in the final international break before the 2026 World Cup

View 2 Images England players celebrate Bukayo Saka's goal against Serbia (Image: Eddie Keogh/The FA)

The Premier League will pause next week as England return to action in the final international break ahead of the World Cup. Thomas Tuchel has just two fixtures remaining to assess his players before naming his squad for Canada, Mexico and the USA in June.

The Three Lions secured their World Cup place with a perfect eight-win qualifying campaign that saw them finish top of their group and arrange another meeting with Croatia in their tournament opener. But beforehand, England are scheduled to play two friendly fixtures at Wembley Stadium as part of the 2026 Send Off Series, where they will welcome Uruguay on March 27 and Japan on March 31.

Tickets for both matches went on general sale in December and February respectively and numerous general admission options remain available alongside more exclusive VIP and hospitality packages. Prices begin at £35 per person at the Wembley Stadium website for a 'Category 4 – Licensed Standing' ticket, with 'Category 1' tickets providing superior views and seating from £80.

VIP and hospitality options for both fixtures are available from £75 at Seat Unique, which offers 'premium' seating on Level Two with padded seats and an excellent view of the pitch. This Taphouse ticket is described as offering 'the ultimate luxury matchday experience' and also grants access to Wembley 2.5 hours before kick-off, plus premium dining, an M&S Foodhall and comfortable seating within the bar area.

England supporters wanting to splash out can reserve a table in the exclusive Bobby Moore Lounge – featuring premium halfway line seating beside the dugout – from £349 per person. This cost also covers a four-course gourmet meal, inclusive bar, live entertainment and unlimited Laurent Perrier Champagne.

Comparable perks come with the more upmarket Bobby Moore Suite, a VIP package with Level 1 seats behind the dugouts from £395 per person. Though tickets are going quickly and only limited availability remains for selected Bobby Moore packages.

View 2 Images (Image: Getty Images)

It's also worth bearing in mind that prices can differ between fixtures. For those planning to watch from home, both England vs Uruguay and England vs Japan will be broadcast live on ITV1, STV, ITVX and STV Player.

The last time England met Uruguay was at the 2014 World Cup, when two goals from then-Liverpool forward Luis Suarez effectively confirmed England's group stage exit. Wayne Rooney's first goal of the tournament wasn't sufficient to prevent their second consecutive defeat as Roy Hodgson's squad ultimately departed early.

Article continues below

England have only played Japan three times, with the most recent meeting coming in 2010 as their final preparation match before travelling to South Africa for the 2010 World Cup. England claimed a 2-1 victory courtesy of two Japan own goals.

England are set to play two additional fixtures in June, taking on New Zealand and Costa Rica in South Florida as part of their pre-World Cup preparations. Ticketing details for these matches haven't been announced yet, though supporters can register their interest through The FA here.

Sports.title: Man Utd news: Scott McTominay shows club what they're missing as midfielder's price unveiled
Sports.content:
Man Utd news: Scott McTominay shows club what they're missing as midfielder's price unveiled

Michael Carrick enjoyed yet another win as Manchester United boss on Sunday as his side bested Aston Villa 3-1 at Old Trafford

View 3 Images Manchester United beat Aston Villa in the Premier League on Sunday (Image: Getty)

Manchester United put Aston Villa to the sword 3-1 at Old Trafford on Sunday afternoon. Michael Carrick has now orchestrated seven wins from nine games this season with Champions League qualification looking all the more likely.

‌



Carrick's men were defeated 2-1 away at Newcastle United in their previous outing and enjoyed a prolonged break between matches thanks to their lack of FA Cup participation. They hosted Unai Emery's side at the weekend and after a goalless first half prevailed 3-1 thanks to strikes from Casemiro, Matheus Cunha and Benjamin Sesko.

‌



A trip to the in-form Bournemouth is next up on their agenda but away from the club, a former player made their long-awaited return. Scott McTominay, who has thrived in Italy with Napoli after leaving United in August 2024, came back from injury on Saturday evening.

‌



The Scottish star was introduced at half-time against Lecce, with Antonio Conte's side trailing 1-0, and he made quite an impact. Mirror Football brings you the latest updates from Old Trafford.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Scott McTominay impresses on return

McTominay had been sidelined since February with tendon inflammation and missed roughly a month with Napoli's medical staff taking a cautious approach. But with his side trailing by a goal, Conte decided to reintroduce the 29-year-old at half-time and his decision paid off.

‌



Barcelona president offers Marcus Rashford update as Man Utd transfer at risk READ MORE :

Michael Carrick stands up for Sir Jim Ratcliffe's Premier League title plan for Man Utd READ MORE :

The midfielder immediately stabilised things in the middle of the park and mere minutes after coming on, Napoli levelled through United loanee Rasmus Hojlund.

Then, in the 68th minute, their comeback was sealed via Matteo Politano. McTominay finished the 2-1 victory with a 90 per cent pass completion rate and reminded United what an influential player they let depart for just £26million.

View 3 Images McTominay returned from injury in the win against Lecce (Image: Getty)

‌



Conte said after the match: "We were losing and I had to make changes. They're strong players but you have to give them time to get used to the pace and intensity again, and that's not easy.

"They need to have confidence, though, because the greater good is Napoli, and since I'm dealing with well-prepared and decent players, there are no problems.

"McTominay had been out for a month. I had to take on some responsibilities, and so did they. The common good, I repeat, is Napoli and we must secure qualification for the Champions League, which is too important. It is important for the top clubs, let alone for us."

‌



Man Utd learn Real Madrid star's price

While McTominay continues to shine at Napoli, another well-regarded midfielder in Europe could seek an exit. Reports suggest Real Madrid star Eduardo Camavinga is a player the club are willing to listen to offers for.

View 3 Images Camavinga could reportedly be on his way out at Real Madrid (Image: Getty)

According to Marca, Los Blancos have slapped a £43m price tag on Camavinga. The Frenchman has been at the Bernabeu since 2021 after signing from Rennes.

Article continues below

But he has struggled for game time of late and it has been suggested that he may soon end up on the chopping block. Several Premier League clubs have allegedly shown an interest and United were originally linked with Camavinga before he headed to Spain nearly five years ago.

Sports.title: Arsenal news: Havertz gets green light for transfer as Arteta shares Dowman plan
Sports.content:
Arsenal news: Havertz gets green light for transfer as Arteta shares Dowman plan

Arsenal are set to face Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League on Tuesday

View 2 Images Kai Havertz is set to face his former side Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League (Image: Vitalii Kliuiev/Getty Images)

Arsenal are preparing for their decisive Champions League game against Bayer Leverkusen on Tuesday night. The Gunners have already turned their attention back to European competition after extending their lead at the top of the Premier League.

‌



Mikel Arteta ’s side are set to host Leverkusen in the return leg of their round of 16 tie. Kai Havertz will be fighting to retain his place and start against his old club, having scored Arsenal’s late equaliser from the penalty spot in the first leg.

‌



The Germany international has proven effective for the Gunners when fit this term and he is still admired by Leverkusen, who sold him to Chelsea in 2020. Havertz was unable to score against Everton at the weekend, though teenage star Max Dowman stepped up to earn Arsenal an important victory.

‌



The 16-year-old became the Premier League’s youngest-ever goalscorer at the weekend and manager Arteta has been asked about his plans for the young midfielder. Here, Mirror Football looks at some of the headlines surrounding the club.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Kai Havertz green light for transfer

Bayer Leverkusen managing director Simon Rolfes has made it clear that Havertz would be welcome back at the club. The forward broke through as a teenager at Leverkusen, making 150 appearances before being sold for around £72million.

‌



Arsenal star Jurrien Timber breaks cover after injury worry that comes at worst time READ MORE :

Max Dowman message sums up Arsenal feelings as John Terry and Rio Ferdinand react READ MORE :

“I’m generally sceptical about bringing former players back. But with Kai [Havertz] and Florian Wirtz, I would make an exception,” Rolfes said on Sport1 programme Doppelpass.

Speaking about Havertz, Rolfes continued: “He spent ten years in our youth system. We maintain close contact with Kai and if the situation arises, we would need to have a conversation.

“At the moment, however, he is in a different phase of his career and feels very comfortable at Arsenal. He left the club to reach the top of world football.”

‌



View 2 Images Max Dowman scored his first Arsenal goal at the weekend (Image: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

Mikel Arteta shares Max Dowman plan

Dowman wrote himself into the history books with his goal against Everton. The Arsenal starlet’s performance generated huge attention and he could be in line for more minutes as the Gunners compete on four fronts.

The 16-year-old made his Champions League debut as a substitute against Slavia Prague in November. However, Arteta called for calm following his latest eye-catching display.

Article continues below

When asked if Dowman could start the team’s next league game, the Arsenal boss responded: "Let's come down to earth today and enjoy the moment, because it was intense and it was worth it and we will see tomorrow."
